sn11.14:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha in the Bamboo Forest, the Squirrels’ Sanctuary.
sn11.14:1.4: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to the Blessed One.
sn11.14:2.1: “Monks, once there was a man living in this very Rājagaha—a poor, pitiful wretch of a person.
sn11.14:2.2: He undertook conviction in the Dhamma & Vinaya proclaimed by the Blessed One, undertook virtue, undertook learning, undertook relinquishment, undertook discernment.
sn11.14:2.3: He—having undertaken conviction in the Dhamma & Vinaya proclaimed by the Blessed One, having undertaken virtue, having undertaken learning, having undertaken relinquishment, having undertaken discernment—on the breakup of the body, after death, reappeared in a good destination, a heavenly world, in the company of the devas of the Thirty-three.
sn11.14:2.4: There he outshone the other devas in beauty & in rank.
sn11.14:2.5: “Then the devas of the Thirty-three were indignant, annoyed, & complained:
sn11.14:2.6: ‘Isn’t it amazing, good sirs! Isn’t it astounding!
sn11.14:2.7: Before, this young deva, when he was a human being, was a poor, pitiful wretch of a person.
sn11.14:2.8: But now, with the breakup of the body, after death, he has reappeared in a good destination, a heavenly world, in the company of the devas of the Thirty-three.
sn11.14:2.9: There he outshines the other devas in beauty & in rank.’
sn11.14:3.1: “Then Sakka the deva-king addressed the devas of the Thirty-three,
sn11.14:3.2: ‘Dear sirs, don’t be indignant with this young deva.
sn11.14:3.3: Before, this young deva, when he was a human being, undertook conviction in the Dhamma & Vinaya proclaimed by the Blessed One, undertook virtue, undertook learning, undertook relinquishment, undertook discernment.
sn11.14:3.4: He—having undertaken conviction in the Dhamma & Vinaya proclaimed by the Blessed One, having undertaken virtue, having undertaken learning, having undertaken relinquishment, having undertaken discernment—on the breakup of the body, after death, has reappeared in a good destination, a heavenly world, in the company of the devas of the Thirty-three.
sn11.14:3.5: There he outshines the other devas in beauty & in rank.’
sn11.14:3.6: “Then, conciliating the devas of the Thirty-three, Sakka the deva-king on that occasion recited these verses:
sn11.14:4.4: appealing to the noble ones, praised;
sn11.14:5.1: who has confidence in the Saṅgha,
sn11.14:6.3: should be cultivated by the intelligent,
